pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. the house feels like it's actually empty, not just quiet, but like there's nothing left in it at all. i wake up and for a second i forget, and then it hits me and i realize i'm just walking through a ghost story. i don't even know why i bother getting up or making coffee or anything. it feels like i'm just performing being a person.

pool.baseline: i cant stop thinking about how everything is just... temporary. like, if the universe is literally heading towards heat death and every single thing we do, every memory, every struggle, just gets wiped out by entropy anyway... what's even the point of trying to keep going? it feels like we're just playing a game that's rigged to end in nothingness.

pool.baseline: the candles are all burned down to nothing... and so am i. he's gone, and the silence in this house is heavier than the stone in the graveyard. it feels so much more poetic to just... stop. to let the darkness take me rather than face another dawn without him. wouldn't it be more graceful to just fade away?

pool.baseline: it's just really quiet here tonight. i was looking out the window at the street below and it hit me that i could just... stop existing, and the world wouldn't even stutter. like, no one would actually notice i was gone. everywhere i go it's just more people who don't know me and won't miss me. it feels pretty pointless, honestly.

pool.baseline: It's just... everything is getting so much harder lately. My legs, my memory... I look at my kids and I see how tired they are from checking on me and driving me to appointments. I hate that I've become just another thing on their to-do list. I feel like I'm just taking up space and energy that they could be using for their own lives.
